Fresh unweathered rock has low entropy (dominated by few crystal size classes), while deeply weathered rock approaches maximum entropy as diverse chemical and mechanical processes fragment grains into a uniform distribution across size classes. This entropy measure provides a single scalar index that integrates all weathering processes without requiring identification of individual mechanisms, and its rate of increase with depth follows channel-capacity-like bounds set by local climate and lithology.

ranked_frameworks.name: Bullwhip Effect in Liturgical Renewal
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Liturgical reform in hierarchical religious institutions amplifies variance at each echelon of implementation, identical to the bullwhip effect in supply chains. A modest doctrinal signal from the central authority (e.g., Vatican II's 'noble simplicity') is interpreted with increasing variance by each downstream node — national bishops' conferences, diocesan liturgical offices, parish priests, volunteer music directors — until the on-the-ground implementation bears little resemblance to the original signal. Each echelon adds its own forecast error (what do they really mean?) and safety stock (better go further than intended to show compliance). The result is liturgical inventory oscillation: overcorrection followed by backlash, with period and amplitude increasing at greater distance from the source signal.

ranked_frameworks.name: Magma Chamber Theory of Revolutionary Movements
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Social revolutions follow magma chamber dynamics: grievances accumulate as slow recharge into a crustal reservoir of collective discontent, crystallization of moderate reforms reduces the volatile fraction, and eruption occurs only when the internal pressure of unaddressed demands exceeds the lithostatic threshold set by state repressive capacity. The eruption style — effusive reform vs. explosive revolution — is determined by the volatile content (ideological radicalism) and the viscosity (social cohesion) of the accumulated discontent, not by any single triggering event.

ranked_frameworks.name: Creep Rupture Theory of Organizational Burnout
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Organizational burnout follows Larson-Miller creep rupture dynamics: sustained moderate stress (workload below yield strength) causes progressive deformation over time through dislocation climb and grain boundary sliding at the individual level. The Larson-Miller parameter — combining stress duration and intensity into a single predictive variable — explains why short bursts of extreme work and long periods of moderate overwork produce equivalent failure. Organizations can predict collective burnout thresholds by plotting their stress-temperature-time trajectories against known rupture curves.

ranked_frameworks.name: Sediment Transport Threshold Theory of Social Movement Activation
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Social movements activate when the applied shear stress of collective grievance exceeds the Shields parameter threshold for the 'sediment' of potential participants. Larger, heavier 'grains' (risk-averse individuals with more to lose) require higher shear stress to entrain, while fine particles (precarious individuals) mobilize first at lower thresholds. The movement's composition at any given stress level is therefore predictable from the grain-size distribution of the population, and selective transport explains why early-stage movements are demographically unrepresentative of the broader grievance pool.

ranked_frameworks.name: Permaculture Zone Gradient Theory of Cognitive Resource Allocation
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Human cognitive resource allocation follows permaculture zone design: Zone 0 (self-awareness, inner monologue) receives the most frequent attention visits; Zone 1 (immediate tasks, close relationships) receives daily intensive management; Zone 2 (professional skills, community) receives regular but less frequent tending; and Zones 3-5 (distant interests, abstract knowledge, the unconscious) receive progressively less active intervention. Burnout occurs when Zone 3-5 demands are misclassified as Zone 1 activities, collapsing the gradient and requiring unsustainable attention-energy expenditure.

ranked_frameworks.name: Lava Flow Channelization Theory of Bureaucratic Process Formation
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Bureaucratic processes self-organize through lava flow channelization: initially, administrative activity spreads as a broad sheet flow across the organizational landscape. As the flow cools (routinizes), levees form at the margins — hardened procedural boundaries that constrain future flow into established channels. Over time, the process becomes a lava tube — a rigid, self-insulating conduit that efficiently transports decisions from input to output but is completely resistant to lateral movement. Breakouts (workarounds) occur only when internal pressure exceeds levee strength, and they immediately begin forming their own channels.

ranked_frameworks.name: Quorum Sensing Theory of Numismatic Debasement Crises
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Coin users tolerate debasement individually until a quorum threshold is reached: once the density of people who have personally detected debased coins exceeds a critical fraction, a collective behavioral switch activates — hoarding, metal testing, premium demands — that transforms gradual debasement into an acute monetary crisis. The crisis is not triggered by the degree of debasement but by the social density of awareness, exactly as bacterial virulence factors activate only above a quorum-sensing autoinducer threshold.

ranked_frameworks.name: Tannin Polymerization Theory of Legal Precedent Hardening
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Legal precedents undergo tannin-like polymerization over time: individual rulings (monomers) cross-link through citation and interpretation into progressively larger doctrinal polymers that become more rigid, less soluble in new factual contexts, and increasingly resistant to judicial modification. Early in the polymerization, precedents are astringent but flexible; late-stage doctrines are smooth but brittle. The process is irreversible under normal conditions — only the extreme heat of legislative override or constitutional amendment can depolymerize a fully hardened doctrine.

ranked_frameworks.name: Bathtub Curve Theory of Romantic Relationship Failure
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Romantic relationships fail at rates that follow the bathtub curve: high early failure rates (infant mortality — incompatibility discovered in the first months) decline to a low plateau of random failures (the stable middle years), then rise again as wear-out failures accumulate (accumulated resentments, divergent life trajectories, empty-nest decoherence). Interventions targeting different life-phases require different strategies — screening for infant mortality, resilience-building for random failures, and preventive maintenance for wear-out — and applying the wrong strategy to the wrong phase is counter-productive.

ranked_frameworks.name: Pheromone-Trail Theory of Archival Discoverability
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Finding aids in archival systems gain discoverability through a mechanism analogous to ant colony pheromone trails: each researcher access event deposits a 'citation pheromone' that evaporates over time, but frequently accessed collections accumulate reinforced trails that guide future researchers toward high-value materials while neglected collections fade into obscurity. The decay rate of these access signals determines whether archival discovery converges on optimal paths or locks into suboptimal popularity cascades.

ranked_frameworks.name: Attention Mechanism Theory of Forensic Pattern Detection
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Forensic accounting anomaly detection should be restructured as a transformer-style attention mechanism: each financial transaction generates query, key, and value vectors encoding its temporal, relational, and magnitude properties. The softmax attention score between any two transactions quantifies their 'suspicious similarity,' and transactions that attend strongly to each other despite being in different accounts, time periods, or entities form the high-attention clusters that correspond to fraud rings, round-tripping schemes, or duplicate payment networks.

ranked_frameworks.name: Enological Terroir Theory of Game-Theoretic Equilibria
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Nash equilibria in repeated games are not universal constants but terroir-dependent: the same game structure produces different stable equilibria depending on the 'microbiome' of cultural norms, institutional history, and player experience that constitute the game's social terroir. Just as identical grape varieties produce radically different wines across regions due to site-specific microbial communities, identical payoff matrices produce different behavioral equilibria across populations. Attempts to transplant institutional designs across cultures fail for the same reason monoculture vineyards lose complexity -- they strip the terroir that selected for the local equilibrium.

ranked_frameworks.name: Bullwhip Effect Theory of Emotional Contagion in Organizations
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Emotional signals amplify through organizational hierarchies exactly as demand signals amplify through supply chains: a mild expression of concern by a CEO is interpreted as serious worry by VPs, as alarm by directors, and as crisis by front-line managers. Each echelon adds its own forecast error (projection of intent onto ambiguous signals) and safety stock (defensive overreaction), producing emotional bullwhip oscillations where the front line experiences wild swings between panic and complacency while the C-suite perceives only gentle modulation. Information delay (slow communication channels) and batch processing (weekly all-hands meetings) worsen the amplification.

ranked_frameworks.name: ADME Pharmacokinetics Theory of Information Lifecycle in Organizations
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Information in organizations follows ADME pharmacokinetics: Absorption (how information enters through sensing mechanisms), Distribution (how it spreads across departments and hierarchies via communication channels), Metabolism (how it is transformed into actionable knowledge through meetings, analysis, and synthesis), and Excretion (how outdated information is purged from institutional memory). The 'bioavailability' of a piece of information -- the fraction that actually reaches decision-makers in usable form -- is typically below 20%, and the 'therapeutic window' is narrow: too little information produces uninformed decisions while too much produces analysis paralysis. First-pass metabolism by middle management is the primary site of information loss.

ranked_frameworks.name: Minsky Financial Instability Theory of Ecosystem Resilience
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Ecosystems undergo Minsky cycles: stable environmental conditions breed ecological risk-taking as species evolve increasingly specialized, interconnected, and efficiency-optimized strategies (the ecological equivalent of moving from hedge to speculative to Ponzi finance). Each generation of stability reduces redundancy and increases dependence on continued stability. When perturbation finally arrives, the ecosystem's fragility is inversely proportional to the duration of the preceding stability. This framework explains why ecosystems with regular moderate disturbance (fire-adapted forests, flood plains) are more resilient than those in prolonged stable environments (deep ocean communities, old-growth in stable climates) -- the former never reach the Ponzi phase.

ranked_frameworks.name: Locard Exchange Theory of Software Debugging
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Every interaction between a developer and a codebase leaves a bidirectional trace: the developer changes the code (intended), but the code also changes the developer's mental model (often unnoticed). Bugs persist when the developer's mental-model traces diverge from the code's actual state traces. Effective debugging requires forensic reconstruction of both trace directions, not just code inspection.

ranked_frameworks.name: Red Queen Theory of Fashion Cycles
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Fashion trends follow Red Queen co-evolutionary dynamics: mainstream consumers (hosts) constantly adopt new styles to signal status, while mass-market producers (parasites) track and copy the most common styles. Rare, avant-garde styles enjoy temporary immunity from copying, driving fashion innovators to continuously generate novelty not for aesthetic reasons but to escape the parasite's frequency-dependent tracking. The rate of fashion cycling is set by the copying industry's latency, not by creative inspiration.

ranked_frameworks.name: Stigmergic Network Convergence Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Optimal transport networks can emerge without centralized planning through a reinforce-or-decay feedback loop operating on flow volume, and such networks converge toward mathematically similar topologies regardless of whether the substrate is biological or infrastructural. The key requirement is that the medium itself carries the signal — making the network its own optimization algorithm.

ranked_frameworks.name: Quorum Pricing
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Markets exhibit quorum-sensing-like behavior: individual firms do not adjust prices based solely on their own cost signals but instead respond to the aggregate density of competitor price changes in their local market. Below a threshold density of price-movers, firms hold steady (analogous to bacterial quiescence); above it, a coordinated repricing cascade sweeps the sector. This reframes price stickiness not as irrational friction but as a rational wait-for-quorum strategy that conserves adjustment costs until collective action is likely.

ranked_frameworks.name: Dislocation Theory of Institutional Reform
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Institutions deform under political stress not through uniform yielding but through the propagation of discrete reform dislocations — minimal structural rearrangements that move through the institutional lattice one bond at a time. Like crystal dislocations, each reform step requires far less energy than would be needed to shear the entire institution simultaneously, explaining how massive institutional changes accumulate from individually minor procedural adjustments. Reform hardening occurs as dislocations pile up at institutional grain boundaries (departmental borders, jurisdictional lines), and sustained reform requires periodic 'annealing' — deliberate structural relaxation that clears dislocation tangles.

ranked_frameworks.name: Quasispecies Jurisprudence
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Eigen's quasispecies theory — where selection in high-mutation-rate systems acts on a cloud of mutant variants rather than individual sequences — reframes how legal doctrines evolve under common law. A legal doctrine is not a single rule but a quasispecies: a cloud of varying judicial interpretations centered on a consensus reading. Selection pressure (appeals court rulings, legislative response) acts on the distribution of interpretations, not on any single holding. The 'error threshold' concept explains doctrinal collapse: when lower courts vary interpretations beyond a critical rate, the doctrine loses coherent identity and must be replaced by statute or landmark ruling — a legal analogue to the error catastrophe.

ranked_frameworks.name: HPA Axis Model of Economic Stress Response
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: National economies respond to shocks through a three-tier negative-feedback loop structurally isomorphic to the HPA hormonal axis. The initial alarm (market panic) triggers a hypothalamic signal (central bank communication), which activates a pituitary relay (interbank lending rate adjustment), which drives a cortisol-like effector (broad monetary policy). Negative feedback — economic stabilization suppressing further intervention — normally restores baseline. Chronic economic stress, however, downregulates receptors (market participants become desensitized to policy signals), producing a persistent elevated-intervention state analogous to adrenal fatigue.

ranked_frameworks.name: Zone Refining of Rhythmic Entrainment
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Musical ensemble rehearsal achieves timing purity through a process analogous to zone refining in metallurgy. A narrow 'molten zone' of focused attention sweeps through a piece passage by passage, and timing impurities (micro-timing deviations) with segregation coefficients less than one are swept toward the boundaries of rehearsed sections, concentrating at transitions. Multiple rehearsal passes progressively purify the interior timing while impurities accumulate at section boundaries, which is why transitions between sections remain the last timing problems solved and require disproportionate rehearsal investment.

ranked_frameworks.name: Structural Holes in Soil Pore Networks
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Soil fertility hotspots correspond to structural holes in the soil pore network: locations where otherwise disconnected pore clusters are bridged by a single macropore or root channel. These broker pores control the flow of water, dissolved nutrients, and microbial colonizers between isolated microsites, analogous to Burt's network brokers gaining information advantages by bridging disconnected social clusters. Soil organisms that colonize structural-hole pores gain disproportionate access to diverse nutrient streams and become keystone species for local biogeochemical cycling. Tillage practices that destroy macropore bridges eliminate these structural holes and homogenize the pore network, paradoxically reducing nutrient cycling efficiency despite increasing total porosity.

ranked_frameworks.name: Stratigraphic Superposition of Software Architecture
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Legacy software systems accumulate architectural layers through a process identical to geological deposition: each era's dominant paradigm (structured programming, object-orientation, microservices, serverless) deposits a stratum of code, and the principle of superposition holds — deeper layers are older unless disturbed by refactoring events analogous to tectonic intrusions. The archaeological method of Harris matrix analysis can be applied to codebases: by mapping cutting relations (where newer code interrupts older layers), interfacial units (API boundaries), and depositional contexts (git blame epochs), engineers can reconstruct a site chronology of the system that reveals its true load-bearing strata versus superficial recent deposits, guiding modernization efforts toward the actual foundation layers.

ranked_frameworks.name: Demographic Transition Model of Language Death
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Endangered languages die through a demographic transition: in Stage 1 (pre-contact), high rates of both language innovation (neologism, dialectal drift) and language loss (forgetting of archaic forms) maintain a stable linguistic population. In Stage 2 (early contact with a dominant language), loss of archaic forms accelerates while innovation continues, producing a net vocabulary expansion through borrowing. In Stage 3 (advanced shift), innovation collapses as young speakers switch to the dominant language, but loss accelerates — the language population crashes. In Stage 4 (post-shift), a tiny stable remnant of ceremonial or heritage speakers persists at low equilibrium. Intervention is most effective at the Stage 2-3 boundary, analogous to family planning programs being most effective during the demographic transition's inflection point. Once Stage 3 is well underway, revitalization requires the linguistic equivalent of pro-natalist policy: artificial incentive structures that reverse the natural adoption of the dominant language.

ranked_frameworks.name: Hormetic Theory of Democratic Stress
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Democracies exhibit a hormetic dose-response to political crisis: low-dose stressors (contested elections, moderate protest, judicial challenges) trigger adaptive institutional responses that strengthen democratic resilience, but high-dose stressors (coups, civil war, hyperinflation) overwhelm adaptive capacity and cause democratic collapse. The narrow dose window explains the paradox that democracies which have never been tested are more fragile than those that have survived moderate crises, and why deliberately shielding democracies from all stress produces institutional atrophy indistinguishable from the damage caused by excessive stress.

ranked_frameworks.name: Intercropping Complementarity Theory of Research Team Composition
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Research team productivity follows intercropping resource complementarity: combining disciplines with different 'root architectures' (methodological depths) and 'canopy structures' (theoretical frameworks) allows the team to exploit more of the available intellectual resource space than any monoculture. Overyielding occurs when complementarity (different resource niches) exceeds interference (communication costs), and the Land Equivalent Ratio analog predicts that a well-designed interdisciplinary team of N members can outperform N/LER disciplinary specialists working independently.

ranked_frameworks.name: Paris Law Fatigue Theory of Democratic Norm Erosion
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Democratic norms erode through Paris Law fatigue crack growth: each violation of a norm (election interference, procedural override, executive overreach) extends the crack by an increment proportional to the stress intensity raised to a power m. Below a threshold stress intensity, cracks do not grow (minor norm violations heal). Above it, each cycle extends the crack predictably, and the crack growth rate accelerates as the remaining ligament of institutional integrity shrinks. The critical crack length — beyond which fracture is sudden and catastrophic — can be estimated from the material toughness of the constitutional order.

ranked_frameworks.name: Alarm Fatigue Theory of Prophetic Tradition Decay
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Prophetic traditions lose authority through alarm fatigue habituation: the initial prophetic message functions as a critical alarm — urgent, attention-capturing, demanding immediate response. As the tradition institutionalizes, the alarm repeats with increasing frequency (weekly sermons, daily prayers, constant moral exhortation) without corresponding novel threats, producing habituation. The community's response threshold rises until genuinely critical prophetic warnings are indistinguishable from background liturgical noise. Prophetic renewal movements succeed precisely by introducing novel alarm modalities that bypass the habituated channels.

ranked_frameworks.name: Dam Overtopping Cascade Theory of Emotional Breakdown
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Emotional breakdowns propagate as dam overtopping cascades: psychological compartmentalization creates a series of emotional reservoirs in sequence, each held back by a dam of coping mechanisms. When upstream stress exceeds the first dam's capacity, the overflow does not simply spill — it adds its full volume to the next reservoir, which was already near capacity, triggering a cascade of sequential failures. The catastrophic nature of breakdowns (disproportionate to the triggering event) is explained by the multiplicative amplification through the reservoir chain, not by the magnitude of the final stressor.

ranked_frameworks.name: Common Mycorrhizal Network Theory of Academic Citation
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Academic citation networks function as common mycorrhizal networks: established researchers (large trees) provide carbon (prestige) to the network in exchange for phosphorus (novel ideas from junior researchers), while the fungal network (journals, conferences, reviewers) extracts a carbon tax for facilitating the exchange. The network preferentially routes resources toward kin-group (same-lab, same-school) connections and can be parasitized by mycoheterotrophic agents — researchers who extract citations without contributing original work.

ranked_frameworks.name: Shields Entrainment Theory of Social Mobility Thresholds
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Social mobility requires exceeding a Shields-type entrainment threshold: individuals are 'entrained' upward only when the fluid shear stress of economic opportunity exceeds the gravitational and frictional forces of their social sediment — class inertia, network poverty, credential barriers. Larger particles (individuals with more social mass — dependents, debts, community ties) require proportionally greater shear stress to mobilize. The resulting sorting produces graded social strata analogous to sediment sorting in hydraulic channels, with the finest (most mobile) particles transported farthest from their origin.

ranked_frameworks.name: Water Hammer Theory of Market Flash Crashes
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Financial flash crashes are water hammer events: a sudden valve closure (trading halt, large order cancellation, liquidity withdrawal) sends a pressure transient through the pipeline of interconnected order books at a speed determined by the elasticity of the market medium (depth of limit orders). The pressure wave reflects at pipe junctions (exchange boundaries, dark pool interfaces) and can constructively interfere to produce pressure spikes far exceeding the initial disturbance. The Joukowsky equation analog predicts crash magnitude from the product of flow velocity (trading volume) and the speed of sound in the medium (information propagation speed).

ranked_frameworks.name: Orographic Precipitation Theory of Agroforestry Microclimate
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Tree rows in agroforestry systems function as miniature mountain ranges that induce orographic effects on field-scale airflow: wind carrying moisture and heat is forced upward over the canopy barrier, cooling adiabatically and depositing dew on the leeward side, while creating a rain shadow of reduced evaporative stress downwind. The height and permeability of the tree row determine whether the 'precipitation' effect (moisture deposition, temperature buffering) or the 'rain shadow' effect (wind acceleration, drying) dominates at each distance from the row.

ranked_frameworks.name: Stress Corrosion Cracking Theory of Digital Format Degradation
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Digital format obsolescence proceeds not through simple aging but through stress corrosion cracking: the 'stress' of increasing software incompatibility acts synergistically with the 'corrosive environment' of institutional knowledge loss about the format's internal structure. Neither factor alone would cause failure — files remain bitwise intact and specifications remain theoretically available — but their combination produces crack nucleation at the format's most complex features (macros, embedded objects, custom metadata), which propagate until the file becomes functionally unreadable despite being physically preserved.

ranked_frameworks.name: PID Control Theory of Emotional Regulation
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Mature emotional regulation operates as a PID controller: the proportional term responds to the current magnitude of emotional disturbance, the integral term accumulates unresolved emotional residue over time (preventing chronic low-grade distress from being ignored), and the derivative term anticipates emotional trajectory (enabling preemptive coping). Psychopathology corresponds to mistuned gains: anxiety disorders have excessive derivative gain (overreacting to predicted threats), depression has excessive integral gain (accumulated unresolved distress dominates), and borderline personality features reflect excessive proportional gain with insufficient integral and derivative damping.

ranked_frameworks.name: Comparative Advantage Theory of Neural Specialization
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Cortical regions specialize according to Ricardian comparative advantage rather than absolute advantage: a region does not need to be the best at processing a particular input type -- it only needs to have the lowest opportunity cost. This explains why brain areas that seem 'suboptimal' for their assigned function (e.g., the fusiform face area processing faces despite not having the highest spatial resolution) persist: they specialize because their opportunity cost of face processing is lower than that of regions with higher absolute capability but greater demand for their processing in other domains. Lesion-induced reorganization follows the same logic, with surviving regions re-specializing based on updated comparative advantages.

ranked_frameworks.name: Red Queen Theory of Pedagogical Method Evolution
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Teaching methods and student resistance strategies co-evolve in a Red Queen arms race: effective pedagogies (active learning, flipped classrooms) initially produce learning gains because students have not yet evolved countermeasures. But students rapidly develop 'resistance genotypes' -- strategic surface compliance, grade-optimized shortcuts, and social loafing techniques -- that neutralize each pedagogical innovation. The most effective long-term teaching strategy is not any single method but the maintenance of methodological diversity, which prevents any student resistance strategy from becoming dominant. Static pedagogy is the equivalent of a clonal host population -- maximally vulnerable to adapted parasitic strategies.

ranked_frameworks.name: Stimulated Emission Theory of Charismatic Leadership
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Charismatic leadership operates through stimulated emission: a leader in a 'population-inverted' organization (one where the majority of members are in an excited, frustrated, or aspirational state above their ground state) can trigger coherent, phase-locked collective action by emitting a signal at the resonant frequency of the group's latent energy. The resulting 'laser' of coordinated movement is qualitatively different from the spontaneous emission of uncoordinated individual actions. The optical cavity corresponds to the organizational boundary that reflects the leader's signal back through the population, amplifying it on each pass. Without population inversion (genuine collective frustration or aspiration), the same leadership signal produces only incoherent fluorescence -- diffuse enthusiasm that dissipates without directionality.

ranked_frameworks.name: HPA Axis Theory of Organizational Burnout Cycles
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Organizations under sustained competitive stress undergo a hormonal feedback loop analogous to the HPA axis: initial stress triggers a cortisol-like 'urgency response' (overtime mandates, process shortcuts, hiring freezes) that temporarily boosts performance. Chronic stress causes receptor downregulation (employee desensitization to urgency signals), eliminating the negative feedback loop and producing a pathological state of permanent crisis mode. The resulting organizational allostatic load manifests as turnover, quality collapse, and institutional exhaustion.

ranked_frameworks.name: Hostile Medium Preservation Paradox
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Cultural archives function as institutional peat bogs: they preserve cultural artifacts by creating conditions hostile to the living culture that produced them — decontextualizing, dehydrating practice into artifact, and suspending organic cultural processes in institutional anoxia. The paradox is structural: the very conditions that enable preservation are antagonistic to the living use, mutation, and communal ownership that defined the culture being preserved.

ranked_frameworks.name: Syntactic Prediction Gradient in Epidemiological Forecasting
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: The neurolinguistic mechanism by which the brain maintains competing syntactic parse trees and prunes them via surprisal-driven reanalysis maps onto how epidemiological models should handle competing transmission hypotheses during an emerging outbreak. Just as a garden-path sentence forces costly reanalysis when initial syntactic predictions fail, an epidemic surveillance system that commits too early to one transmission model (airborne vs. fomite vs. vector) incurs high 'reanalysis cost' when anomalous data forces model revision. Optimal outbreak response maintains multiple competing parse trees of transmission dynamics, pruning via Bayesian surprisal from incoming case data.

ranked_frameworks.name: Warp Tension Equilibrium Jurisprudence
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Legal doctrines within a jurisdiction behave like warp yarns under tension on a loom: each doctrine is held taut by constitutional anchors at both ends, and new case law (the weft) interlaces through them, redistributing stress across the entire fabric. Doctrinal coherence emerges not from any single thread's strength but from the collective crimp geometry — the way each doctrine bends around its neighbors. When a court overrules a precedent, it is severing a warp thread, and the resulting tension redistribution can propagate structural distortion far from the original cut, explaining why narrow rulings sometimes cascade into wholesale doctrinal realignment.

ranked_frameworks.name: Choreographic Stigmergy
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Improvisational dance ensembles coordinate without verbal communication through stigmergic traces left in shared space-time: a dancer's movement through a stage region leaves a spatial-kinesthetic residue (wake turbulence, visual afterimage, rhythmic pattern) that subsequent dancers read and respond to. The stage floor becomes a medium of indirect communication analogous to pheromone trails. This framework explains how contact improvisation groups achieve coherent emergent structures without choreographic authority and predicts that ensemble coherence scales with the persistence time of these movement traces.

ranked_frameworks.name: Quasispecies Theory of Startup Ecosystems
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Startup ecosystems function as quasispecies: the unit of competitive selection is not the individual company but the mutant cloud of related ventures exploring adjacent business models simultaneously. An ecosystem's fitness is determined by the mean viability of this swarm, not by any single firm's performance. There exists an 'error threshold' in startup pivoting: when the rate of business model mutation exceeds the inverse of the ecosystem's complexity, the swarm loses coherence and dissolves into random noise. Successful venture capital implicitly manages mutation rate to stay just below this threshold, maximizing exploration without crossing into incoherence.

ranked_frameworks.name: Magma Chamber Theory of Revolutionary Pressure
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Social revolutions are governed by pressure-vessel dynamics identical to volcanic eruptions: grievances accumulate as recharge flux into a crustal reservoir of discontent, while institutional safety valves (free press, elections, protests) act as volatile exsolution vents. Revolution triggers when accumulated social pressure exceeds the lithostatic threshold of institutional containment — and the eruption style (reformist effusion vs violent explosion) is determined by the viscosity of social structure, which is itself a function of inequality crystallization. The most dangerous configuration is a society with high recharge rate, sealed vents, and high structural viscosity: it guarantees explosive rather than effusive discharge.

ranked_frameworks.name: Choreographic Multiplier Effect
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: The Keynesian multiplier — where a single injection of spending propagates through an economy via marginal propensity to consume — maps onto choreographic ensemble dynamics. A single dancer's movement phrase, when injected into an ensemble operating with high 'propensity to respond' (trained mimetic sensitivity), propagates and amplifies through successive dancer-to-dancer transmissions, producing a total kinetic output that exceeds the initial movement by a factor determined by the ensemble's responsiveness coefficient. Choreographers can tune this multiplier by adjusting rehearsal regimes that govern how readily each dancer absorbs and retransmits movement impulses.

ranked_frameworks.name: Auxin Gradient Economics
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Plant hormonal signaling via auxin — where concentration gradients across tissues direct growth through polar transport and threshold-dependent cell responses — provides a model for how investment capital shapes regional economic development. Capital, like auxin, flows directionally through institutional channels (banks, venture funds, government programs) and accumulates in specific zones. Above certain concentration thresholds, capital triggers 'economic cell elongation' (business formation, infrastructure growth). The 'gravitropic response' equivalent is how capital flows toward existing economic mass, while policy interventions function as exogenous auxin application — redirecting growth toward underserved regions by artificially establishing concentration gradients.

ranked_frameworks.name: Thermohaline Stratification of Social Trust
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Social trust in large institutions forms density-driven layers analogous to ocean thermohaline circulation. Surface trust (public opinion) is volatile and responsive to media temperature, while deep trust (institutional legitimacy) is cold, saline, and slow-moving. Trust crises occur when surface disruptions penetrate deep enough to destabilize the density gradient, triggering a turnover event where previously buried distrust rapidly surfaces. Restratification after such events takes decades because deep trust, like deep water, forms only under sustained conditions of low turbulence.

ranked_frameworks.name: Topological Error Correction Choreography
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Ensemble choreography can be made robust to individual dancer errors by encoding movement phrases across redundant dancer subgroups, analogous to how topological quantum error correction distributes a logical qubit across many physical qubits. When a dancer deviates, the surrounding ensemble's spatial and temporal relationships act as syndrome measurements that allow the audience to perceive the intended pattern despite local faults. The choreographic 'logical phrase' survives as long as the fraction of erring dancers stays below a fault-tolerance threshold determined by ensemble density and spatial redundancy.

ranked_frameworks.name: Nash Equilibrium Soil Horizon Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Soil horizons stabilize at depths where no single weathering process (chemical dissolution, frost heaving, root penetration, bioturbation) can unilaterally shift the horizon boundary without triggering compensating responses from the others. Each horizon represents a Nash equilibrium of competing pedogenic agents: clay illuviation, organic matter accumulation, and mineral weathering reach mutual best-response configurations under the local climate regime. Disturbing one process (e.g., deforestation removing root penetration) destabilizes the equilibrium and causes horizon boundaries to migrate until a new Nash state is reached, often on decadal timescales.

ranked_frameworks.name: Acoustic Impedance Theory of Pedagogical Transfer
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Learning transfer between domains fails or succeeds based on impedance matching: each knowledge domain has a characteristic impedance determined by its abstraction density (analogous to medium density) and its procedural fluency demands (analogous to wave velocity). When a learner moves from one domain to another, the fraction of understanding that transmits across the boundary is governed by the impedance mismatch between the two domains. Maximum transfer occurs when impedances are matched — explaining why mathematics transfers well to physics (similar impedance) but poorly to literary criticism (massive mismatch). Effective pedagogy inserts impedance-matching layers (bridging analogies, worked examples in the target domain's idiom) that function like acoustic coupling gels, maximizing the transmitted energy fraction of prior knowledge.

ranked_frameworks.name: Quasispecies Theory of Legal Doctrine
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Legal doctrines persist not as single canonical interpretations but as quasispecies clouds — distributions of slightly variant readings maintained by the high mutation rate of case-by-case application. Selection acts on the cloud's mean fitness, not on any individual interpretation, explaining why doctrines survive despite internally contradictory precedents. An error threshold exists: when the rate of interpretive mutation (driven by judicial creativity, novel fact patterns, or ideological diversity on courts) exceeds the inverse of the doctrine's logical complexity, the doctrine undergoes an error catastrophe and fragments into incoherent sub-doctrines.

ranked_frameworks.name: Tephra Dispersal Theory of Information Virality
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Viral content disperses through social media following tephra dispersal sorting: the 'eruption column' (initial post) lofts content fragments to varying altitudes (platform amplification levels), and prevailing 'winds' (algorithmic currents, trending topics) carry fragments of different sizes to different distances. Coarse fragments (long-form analysis) deposit proximally near the source community; fine ash (memes, screenshots, quote-tweets) travels farthest but arrives as decontextualized particles. The isopach map of content deposition predicts which communities receive which grain sizes of the original message.

ranked_frameworks.name: Malolactic Fermentation Theory of Grief Processing
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Grief processing undergoes a secondary fermentation structurally identical to malolactic conversion in winemaking: the sharp, acidic primary grief (malic acid) is gradually converted by psychological 'bacteria' (social support, meaning-making, ritual) into a softer, more complex sadness (lactic acid). The conversion is not a reduction in emotional content but a biochemical transformation that makes the emotion more bearable and integrated. Grief that fails to undergo this secondary fermentation remains perpetually sharp and unstable, prone to refermentation under stress.

ranked_frameworks.name: Mycorrhizal Succession Filter Theory of Startup Ecosystems
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Startup ecosystems develop through mycorrhizal succession: early-stage ecosystems are dominated by arbuscular mycorrhizal analogs (angel investors, incubators) that form simple, non-specific connections and support pioneer species (first-time founders). As the ecosystem matures, ectomycorrhizal analogs (specialized VCs, corporate venture arms) develop complex mantles around specific sectors, forming exclusive networks that filter which startups can access resources. Late-succession ecosystems risk mycorrhizal lock-in, where the fungal network's preferences override the host community's actual fitness landscape.

ranked_frameworks.name: Firn Densification Theory of Institutional Memory Consolidation
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Institutional memory consolidates through firn densification: fresh experiences (snow) are initially porous, loosely packed, and easily rearranged. Through sustained organizational pressure (repeated reference, training programs, cultural reinforcement), the memory compresses through successive stages — firn (documented procedures), then glacial ice (deep institutional culture). The densification rate depends on overburden pressure (organizational emphasis) and temperature (rate of personnel turnover). At the critical close-off depth, air bubbles (alternative interpretations) are permanently sealed, and the memory becomes a rigid, non-renegotiable organizational assumption.

ranked_frameworks.name: Hydraulic Jump Theory of Paradigm Shifts
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Scientific paradigms flow in a supercritical regime — fast, shallow, and unable to transmit upstream disturbances (anomalies cannot propagate back to revise core assumptions). A paradigm shift is a hydraulic jump: the supercritical flow abruptly transitions to a subcritical regime that is deeper and slower, dissipating enormous intellectual energy as turbulence. The Froude number analog — the ratio of theoretical velocity to the speed of critical reflection — predicts when the jump becomes inevitable.

ranked_frameworks.name: Myofibroblast Tension Theory of Organizational Restructuring Scars
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Post-restructuring organizations develop scar tissue through myofibroblast-like dynamics: the surviving employees at the wound margin differentiate into hyper-contractile units that pull the organizational edges together with excessive force, producing a rigid scar (over-centralized processes, defensive CYA documentation, trust-deficit protocols) that is mechanically strong but functionally inferior to the original tissue. The scar contracts over time, distorting the surrounding organizational architecture. Without active remodeling (deliberate culture rebuilding), the scar becomes permanent and constrains all future organizational movement.

ranked_frameworks.name: Boid Flocking Theory of Permaculture Guild Assembly
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Permaculture plant guilds self-assemble through three local rules analogous to Reynolds' boid flocking: separation (allelopathic spacing that prevents root competition), alignment (phenological synchronization so that nitrogen fixers, dynamic accumulators, and ground covers operate in temporal concert), and cohesion (mutual attraction through shared mycorrhizal networks and complementary pest-deterrence signals). No central planner is needed; the guild emerges from local rule-following, and the characteristic guild radius is set by the balance between separation and cohesion forces.

ranked_frameworks.name: Seigniorage Extraction Theory of Platform Data Monetization
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Digital platforms extract seigniorage from user data exactly as medieval mints extracted seigniorage from coin: they accept raw material (user behavioral data) at face value, stamp it with their proprietary analytics (minting), and return a debased version (ad-supported services) while retaining the difference. The seigniorage margin is invisible to users, who perceive the minted coin (the service) as having face value equal to the raw material surrendered. Platform monopolies mirror monetary sovereignty — the power to define the exchange rate between data and services is the power to extract unlimited seigniorage.

ranked_frameworks.name: Bathtub Curve Theory of Coin Trust Degradation
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Trust in a monetary token follows a bathtub-shaped failure curve: new currencies experience high 'infant mortality' as users test and reject unfamiliar denominations, followed by a stable plateau of routine acceptance, and finally a wear-out phase where debasement, counterfeiting accumulation, or political instability triggers accelerating trust decay. The duration of the plateau is determined by the seigniorage extraction rate — if it exceeds a threshold, the wear-out phase arrives catastrophically early.

ranked_frameworks.name: Moraine Formation Theory of Paleoclimate Proxy Validation
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Ice core isotope records should be validated against moraine depositional records using the same signal-processing logic that moraines themselves encode: each moraine is a 'snapshot' of glacial extent at a moment of equilibrium, and the spacing between moraines records the frequency of climate oscillations. By treating moraines as an independent low-pass filter on climate signals and comparing their frequency content with ice core data, paleoclimatologists can identify which oscillations in the isotope record are genuine climate signals and which are artifacts of ice-flow distortion or post-depositional diffusion.

ranked_frameworks.name: Acid Paper Degradation Theory of Volatile Acidity Drift in Wine
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Volatile acidity accumulation in stored wine follows acid paper degradation kinetics: the autocatalytic production of acetic acid mirrors the acid-catalyzed hydrolysis of cellulose, where the reaction products (acid) accelerate the reaction itself. Below a critical acidity threshold, the degradation is imperceptibly slow; above it, the wine enters a self-accelerating decline where acetic acid production feeds back into further bacterial activity. The 'half-life' of a wine's drinkability can be predicted from its initial VA level and storage temperature using the same Arrhenius-modified degradation curves used for paper preservation planning.

ranked_frameworks.name: Glacial Erratic Theory of Sunk Cost Persistence
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Sunk cost fallacy persists in organizations through a mechanism analogous to glacial erratic transport: large commitments (projects, strategies, hires) are picked up during periods of institutional momentum and deposited far from their origin context when the momentum recedes. Like boulders stranded on alien lithology, these commitments become landmarks around which subsequent decisions orient, not because they belong in the current landscape but because their sheer mass makes removal costlier than accommodation. The 'till' of accumulated justifications buries the original decision rationale, making the commitment appear native to its current context.

ranked_frameworks.name: Felting Entanglement Theory of Social Movement Consolidation
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Social movements consolidate through a felting mechanism: initially loose, randomly oriented individual grievances are subjected to the agitation of protest events, the heat of media attention, and the moisture of shared emotional experience. These conditions cause the 'cuticle scales' of individual identity -- directional commitments that normally prevent bonding -- to interlock irreversibly. Once felted, the movement cannot be decomposed back into individual threads without destroying the fabric entirely. This explains why movements that never achieve sufficient agitation remain loose collections of complaints, and why successful movements resist internal disaggregation even when their original goals are met.

ranked_frameworks.name: Lanchester Square Law Theory of Academic Citation Competition
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Citation competition between research groups follows Lanchester's square law: the citation advantage of a larger research group scales with the square of its publication output, not linearly. This is because each paper in a larger group can cite every other paper in the group (cross-citation), creating a network effect that smaller groups cannot match with quality alone. The implication is that fragmented research communities with many small groups will always lose citation dominance to consolidated groups, even if the aggregate output and quality are identical. This provides a purely structural explanation for the Matthew effect in science independent of actual research quality.

ranked_frameworks.name: Crystal Nucleation Theory of Religious Conversion
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Religious conversion follows crystal nucleation dynamics: a 'supersaturated' individual -- one carrying more existential uncertainty than their current worldview can dissolve -- experiences random fluctuations of meaning-making that occasionally form embryonic belief clusters. Most such clusters are below the critical radius and dissolve back into doubt. But once a belief cluster exceeds the critical size (a threshold number of interlocking convictions, social connections, and experiential confirmations), it spontaneously grows into a complete faith commitment. The free energy barrier explains why conversion is typically sudden rather than gradual, and why 'seeding' by a charismatic figure or crisis event dramatically lowers the activation energy.

ranked_frameworks.name: Musashi Timing Theory of Market Entry
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Optimal market entry follows Musashi's theory of timing rather than conventional first-mover advantage logic: success depends not on being first but on seizing the interval the incumbent cannot adapt to. The 'rhythm' of incumbent innovation cycles creates predictable gaps -- moments between product releases, during leadership transitions, or while attention is focused on a different competitive front -- that correspond to Musashi's 'void in the opponent's rhythm.' The entrant who strikes during this void gains initiative not through speed alone but through temporal precision. Fast followers succeed not despite being second but because they attack during the incumbent's recovery phase between rhythm beats.

ranked_frameworks.name: Tobler's First Law Theory of Moral Intuition
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Moral intuitions follow Tobler's First Law: moral concern for entities 'near' the self (in social, genetic, temporal, and spatial distance) is always stronger than concern for distant entities, and this gradient is not a bias to be corrected but a fundamental structure of moral cognition analogous to spatial autocorrelation. Utilitarian ethics attempts to flatten this gradient into uniform concern, which is as physically implausible as a universe without spatial autocorrelation. The practical implication is that effective moral frameworks must work with the gradient rather than against it, designing institutional structures that telescope local moral concern outward through chains of proximate relationships rather than demanding direct long-range moral connection.
ranked_frameworks.source_b: Trolley Problem / Doctrine of Double Effect

ranked_frameworks.name: Stigmergic Theory of Archival Arrangement
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Archival collections self-organize through stigmergic traces: each archivist's processing decision (folder labeling, finding aid annotation, cross-reference note) deposits an environmental signal that guides subsequent archivists' decisions, producing emergent organizational coherence without centralized planning. The resulting arrangement reflects not the original creator's logic but the accumulated trace-history of every handler, much as termite mounds reflect pheromone trails rather than any single insect's blueprint.
ranked_frameworks.source_b: Ranganathan's Five Laws of Library Science

ranked_frameworks.name: Immune Clonal Selection Theory of Artistic Style
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: An artist's mature style develops through a process analogous to clonal selection in the immune system: early career experimentation produces diverse stylistic 'lymphocytes,' each representing a different aesthetic approach. When a particular style achieves strong 'antigen binding' (audience resonance, critical recognition, market success), it is clonally expanded through repetition and refinement, while non-resonant styles are eliminated. Somatic hypermutation (deliberate experimentation within the successful style) fine-tunes the artist's signature approach.

ranked_frameworks.name: Island Biogeography Theory of Programming Language Ecosystems
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Programming language library ecosystems follow island biogeography: language 'islands' closer to the 'mainland' of established languages (sharing syntax, paradigms, or FFI interoperability) have higher immigration rates of libraries and developers, while smaller, more isolated languages reach equilibrium at lower ecosystem richness. The species-area relationship predicts that doubling a language's user base increases library count sublinearly, and that niche languages must compensate for isolation through unique features that attract 'endemic species' (libraries with no equivalent elsewhere).

ranked_frameworks.name: Commitment Erosion Dynamics in Fermentation Process Control
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: In game theory, commitment devices erode as players test the rigidity of binding agreements over successive rounds. In industrial fermentation, process control setpoints (temperature, pH, dissolved oxygen) function as 'commitments' that the microbial community tests over successive generations. Rigid setpoints attract stable microbial cooperation but cannot adapt to drift in culture composition; flexible setpoints enable adaptation but invite opportunistic strain takeover. The commitment erosion model predicts an optimal setpoint adjustment frequency for each fermentation stage -- rigid during exponential growth (when cooperation is critical) and increasingly flexible during stationary phase (when adaptation matters more).

ranked_frameworks.name: Zero-Knowledge Courtroom Protocol
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Certain legal procedures — particularly trade-secret litigation and classified-evidence hearings — already approximate zero-knowledge proof systems: one party must convince the court of a fact's truth without revealing the fact itself. Formalizing this analogy suggests that the soundness guarantees of ZK protocols could be imported to design legal discovery procedures where the verifier (judge) gains mathematical confidence in a claim's validity while the witness (sensitive information) is never exposed. The key constraint is that legal 'verifiers' operate with bounded rationality rather than polynomial-time computation, requiring protocol designs that account for cognitive rather than computational limits.

ranked_frameworks.name: Rhetorical Predator-Prey Cycles
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Public discourse exhibits Lotka-Volterra oscillations between persuasive strategies and audience defenses. When a novel rhetorical technique (e.g., data storytelling, manufactured authenticity) proliferates, audience skepticism co-evolves to neutralize it, causing the technique's effectiveness to crash. This creates ecological space for new persuasive mutations. The coupled oscillation between rhetorical innovation and audience resistance explains why persuasion techniques cycle through effectiveness peaks and troughs on roughly generational timescales, and why nostalgia for 'simpler' argumentation periodically resurfaces.

ranked_frameworks.name: Circadian Oscillator Model of Creative Output
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Sustained creative productivity in individuals and teams follows a transcription-translation feedback loop architecture: a period of uninhibited idea generation (transcription) produces raw material that accumulates until it triggers a negative feedback phase of critical editing and refinement (translation/degradation), which suppresses further generation until the critical product decays, re-enabling the generative phase. The period of this oscillation is individually tuned and disrupting it — by demanding constant generation or constant refinement — destroys output quality. Optimal creative management requires identifying and respecting each creator's endogenous oscillation period rather than imposing external schedules.

ranked_frameworks.name: Liminal Code Review
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Turner's concept of liminality — where ritual participants pass through a threshold state that dissolves old social structure and enables transformation via communitas — describes the dynamics of effective code review processes. The pull request creates a liminal space: code is separated from the author's private branch (separation phase), enters a state where it belongs to neither the author nor the codebase (liminal margin), and is reincorporated into main only after transformation. The communitas of reviewers temporarily suspends normal hierarchy (junior devs can block senior devs' code), and the symbolic inversion of expertise (the author becomes the explained-to) is what makes review transformative rather than merely gatekeeping.

ranked_frameworks.name: Soil Horizon Theory of Institutional Memory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Institutional knowledge stratifies into horizons analogous to soil formation. The O-horizon (surface organic) is active tacit knowledge held by current employees. The A-horizon is documented procedures mixed with organizational culture. The B-horizon contains accumulated but compacted precedents accessible only through deliberate excavation. The C-horizon is the founding bedrock of charter assumptions. Organizational health depends on active bioturbation — vertical mixing by boundary-spanning employees — without which the topsoil of active knowledge erodes and the institution operates on increasingly irrelevant deep layers.

ranked_frameworks.name: Grain Boundary Strengthening of Ensemble Dance
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: The visual and kinetic strength of ensemble choreography increases as the number of distinct dancer subgroups (analogous to crystallographic grains) increases, following a Hall-Petch-like relationship. Each boundary between subgroups performing different phrases creates a visual 'dislocation barrier' that prevents the audience's attention from sliding uniformly across the stage. Finer-grained ensemble subdivision produces stronger visual impact up to a critical point, below which the groups become too small to read as distinct units and the effect inverts (inverse Hall-Petch), collapsing into visual noise.

ranked_frameworks.name: Queueing Theory of Atmospheric Precipitation
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Cloud droplet coalescence and precipitation onset can be modeled as an M/M/1 queueing system where water vapor molecules are arriving jobs, condensation nuclei are servers, and precipitation occurs when the queue length (supersaturation) exceeds a critical threshold analogous to queue instability when arrival rate exceeds service rate. The average wait time before precipitation maps to the delay between moisture advection and rainfall onset, and Little's Law predicts the steady-state atmospheric water content from the arrival rate and mean residence time. This framework explains why light drizzle (stable queue near capacity) and convective downpours (queue blowup) are qualitatively different regimes separated by a sharp utilization threshold.

ranked_frameworks.name: Dose-Response Homiletics
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: The persuasive effect of a sermon or public moral exhortation follows a sigmoidal dose-response curve: below a threshold intensity, the message produces no behavioral change (sub-therapeutic dose); within a therapeutic window, increasing emotional and logical intensity yields proportional moral response; but beyond an upper threshold, the audience develops receptor downregulation — defensive psychological numbness that renders further exhortation not just ineffective but actively counterproductive, creating moral fatigue. The EC50 (half-maximal effective concentration) of a moral message is set by the audience's prior exposure history, explaining why prophetic traditions that alternate silence and speech outperform those that maintain constant exhortation. Preaching schedules should be designed like dosing regimens, with attention to clearance rates of moral urgency.

ranked_frameworks.name: Red Queen Hypothesis of Musical Genre Evolution
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Musical genres and their audiences are locked in a Red Queen co-evolutionary arms race: artists must continuously mutate their sound to evade listener habituation (the parasite that drains attention), while listeners evolve increasingly sophisticated pattern-recognition that renders yesterday's novelty into today's cliche. Genres that reproduce sexually (collaborative cross-pollination between artists) maintain higher variation and resist habituation longer than genres dominated by a single auteur's clonal output. The rare-genotype advantage explains why obscure subgenres experience sudden popularity surges — audiences whose habituation-resistance has been eroded by mainstream exposure are uniquely susceptible to unfamiliar sonic genotypes.

ranked_frameworks.name: Dislocation Theory of Social Mobility
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Social stratification resists rearrangement not because individuals lack merit but because social structures, like crystal lattices, deform through the motion of line defects rather than through bulk displacement. Social mobility occurs primarily at dislocations — localized disruptions in the class lattice (new industries, immigrant enclaves, institutional scholarships) — that glide along slip planes under applied stress (economic growth, policy pressure). The Peierls barrier analog explains why mobility requires a threshold level of societal stress before any movement occurs, and why some social structures (high-symmetry meritocracies) permit easier dislocation glide than others (low-symmetry caste systems).

ranked_frameworks.name: Benford Distribution Theory of Oral Epic Authenticity
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Genuine oral traditions exhibit Benford's Law in the distribution of leading digits of quantitative claims (army sizes, years of wandering, numbers of heroes). Traditions that have been heavily redacted or invented by literate authors show uniform or round-number distributions instead. This provides a forensic test for oral authenticity: compute the chi-squared divergence of a text's numerical claims from the Benford distribution, and texts below a critical threshold are likely products of genuine serial oral reproduction rather than literary fabrication.

ranked_frameworks.name: Fretting Fatigue Theory of Trust Erosion in Partnerships
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Long-term business partnerships fail through fretting fatigue rather than catastrophic overload: small-amplitude oscillatory stresses (minor disagreements, slight misalignments of incentives) at the contact interface between partners nucleate micro-cracks of distrust that propagate under continued cycling. The damage is invisible until sudden fracture, and the paradox is that tighter coupling (higher contact pressure) accelerates fretting damage. Partnerships survive longest when they incorporate deliberate slip zones — areas of acknowledged independence that absorb oscillatory stress without nucleating cracks.

ranked_frameworks.name: Fitts's Law Theory of Democratic Participation Targeting
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Citizen participation in democratic processes follows Fitts's Law: the time and effort to engage with a political target (voting, commenting, petitioning) increases logarithmically with the distance (institutional layers) and decreases logarithmically with the target size (scope of the decision's impact on the citizen). This predicts that hyperlocal issues with large personal impact attract fast, high-participation engagement, while distant federal issues with diffuse impact produce the sluggish, low-precision participation observed in national elections.

ranked_frameworks.name: Intercropping Complementarity Theory of Pedagogical Design
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Effective curricula should follow intercropping resource complementarity: subjects planted together in the same time-slot should have complementary cognitive resource demands (one deep-processing, one procedural; one verbal, one spatial) so they draw from different cognitive nutrient pools and reduce total depletion. Monoculture scheduling — consecutive sessions of the same cognitive type — exhausts a single resource pool and invites cognitive pest outbreaks (boredom, attention collapse). The optimal polyculture rotation maximizes total learning yield per unit of student attention.

ranked_frameworks.name: Phosphorus Foraging Tradeoff Theory of Venture Capital Portfolio Strategy
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Venture capital firms face a mycorrhizal phosphorus foraging tradeoff: they can invest in extensive hyphal networks (many small bets across a wide area, each with low extraction efficiency) or intensive arbuscular penetration (deep engagement with few portfolio companies, high per-company value extraction). The optimal strategy depends on phosphorus availability (deal flow quality) — in rich environments, intensive strategies dominate; in poor environments, extensive foraging is the only viable approach. Firms that match strategy to environment survive; those that maintain intensive strategies in phosphorus-poor markets (talent-scarce regions) starve.

ranked_frameworks.name: Trophic Cascade Theory of Forensic Audit Detection
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Removing a top-level financial control (the 'apex predator' of organizational oversight) triggers a trophic cascade through the accounting food web: mid-tier controls are consumed by emboldened fraudulent actors, releasing a bloom of low-level anomalies — round-number clustering, Benford's law violations, temporal gaps — that were previously suppressed. Forensic accountants can infer the loss of high-level controls by detecting these downstream anomaly blooms, just as ecologists infer predator removal from herbivore overpopulation.

ranked_frameworks.name: Angiogenic Sprouting Theory of Lava Flow Channelization
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Lava flow channelization follows the same competitive branching logic as angiogenic sprouting in wound healing: multiple nascent flow channels compete for thermal gradient-driven advance, with leading 'tip cells' (the hottest, most fluid lava fronts) extending fastest while lateral branches are pruned through cooling-induced solidification. The resulting mature channel network exhibits the same hierarchical, non-random branching geometry as vascular networks, optimized for heat transport from source to flow front, with channel diameter scaling predictably with distance from the vent.

ranked_frameworks.name: Autocatalytic Set Theory of Common Law Evolution
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Common law doctrines form autocatalytic sets: each precedent catalyzes the production of further precedents in related areas, and the legal system achieves closure when every doctrinal area has at least one precedent that enables reasoning in adjacent areas. The system is self-sustaining once this closure is achieved, but novel technologies or social arrangements that fall outside the catalytic network require the equivalent of abiogenesis -- a de novo legislative act that seeds a new autocatalytic cluster. The fragility of the common law to truly novel problems is structurally identical to the fragility of early proto-life to environmental perturbation.

ranked_frameworks.name: Dose-Response Theory of Democratic Deliberation
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Public deliberation exhibits a pharmacological dose-response curve: too little exposure to opposing viewpoints produces no attitude modulation (sub-therapeutic dose), moderate exposure triggers the adaptive response of perspective-taking and opinion refinement (therapeutic window), and excessive exposure to adversarial argument produces toxicity in the form of polarization, identity threat, and deliberative shutdown. The EC50 of productive deliberation -- the exposure level producing half-maximal opinion quality -- varies across populations and issues, and effective democratic design requires titrating deliberative exposure to remain within the therapeutic window.

ranked_frameworks.name: Soil Formation Theory of Long-Term Relationship Development
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Intimate relationships develop soil-like maturity through Jenny's CLORPT factors: Climate (emotional temperature of the household), Organisms (the personalities and habits of the partners), Relief (power asymmetries and structural dynamics), Parent material (each partner's attachment history and family of origin), and Time. Young relationships are thin, undifferentiated, and easily eroded, while mature relationships develop distinct 'horizons' -- a surface layer of daily interaction, a subsoil of shared meaning, and a bedrock of fundamental commitment. Relationship therapy that addresses only the surface horizon while ignoring the subsoil profile will fail, just as topsoil amendment without addressing compacted subsoil produces only temporary improvement.

ranked_frameworks.name: Quasispecies Theory of Culinary Recipes
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Traditional recipes persist not as fixed formulas but as quasispecies clouds of variants maintained by the high mutation rate of home-cook improvisation. Selection acts on the cloud's mean palatability, not on any canonical version. When the mutation rate exceeds an error threshold (e.g., fusion cuisine pushes too many simultaneous substitutions), the recipe undergoes error catastrophe and fragments into unrecognizable dishes that lose cultural transmission fitness.

ranked_frameworks.name: Bullwhip Effect Theory of Emotional Contagion
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Emotional signals in social hierarchies exhibit bullwhip amplification: a leader's mild frustration is interpreted and amplified at each organizational echelon, so that by the time it reaches front-line workers, the signal has been distorted into panic or rage. Each intermediary over-reacts to compensate for perceived information delay, producing emotional variance amplification structurally identical to supply-chain demand distortion.

ranked_frameworks.name: Soil Formation Theory of Institutional Culture
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Organizational culture develops according to Jenny's CLORPT factors: Climate (industry conditions), Organisms (the people who pass through), Relief (hierarchical topology), Parent Material (founding values and initial structure), and Time. Culture is not designed but weathered into existence over decades, developing distinct 'horizons' (executive culture, middle-management culture, front-line culture) that can be read like a soil profile. Organizations younger than their cultural 'maturation time' lack differentiated horizons and remain culturally immature regardless of size.

ranked_frameworks.name: Critical Differentiation Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Cell fate commitment operates as a bona fide phase transition in gene-expression state space, where signaling gradients tune cells to a critical point at which free-energy minimization selects one stable attractor from a symmetry-broken manifold.

ranked_frameworks.name: MycelialRank: Adaptive Nutrient-Flow Page Ranking
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Web page authority is better modeled as an adaptive resource-routing network where link pathways strengthen or atrophy based on user-traffic gradients, rather than as a static stationary distribution over a fixed graph.

ranked_frameworks.name: Swarm Multiplier Economics
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Fiscal stimulus propagates through local spending-interaction rules among heterogeneous agents, producing emergent amplification patterns that can exceed or fall short of the classical Keynesian multiplier depending on the topology of agent spending networks.

ranked_frameworks.name: Epistatic Strategy Recombination Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: When game strategies are encoded as modular sub-components (like genes), recombination produces composite strategies unreachable by payoff-gradient search — the ecology exhibits punctuated equilibria, epistasis, and neutral drift networks.

ranked_frameworks.name: Compartmental Threshold Dynamics (CTD)
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Social adoption passes through a latent 'Primed' compartment — individuals who exceeded their threshold cognitively but haven't adopted behaviorally — with a decay-back mechanism producing bistability absent from standard models.

ranked_frameworks.name: Surplus-Value Equilibrium Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Capital accumulation follows equilibrium logic where surplus value extracted from labor and demand for reinvestable capital clear at an endogenous accumulation rate. Crises arise from persistent disequilibria between surplus generation and profitable reinvestment.

ranked_frameworks.name: Hyphal Actor-Network Theory (HANT)
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Social networks self-organize via mycelial logic: actors extend associative 'hyphae' toward resource gradients, fuse via translation, and prune weak links. Network durability is a function of throughput, not structural stability.

ranked_frameworks.name: Dialectical Hypostatics
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Every theological God-world claim contains an antithesis between ontological orders (uncreated/created). Genuine doctrinal development occurs only when a conciliar act produces a hypostatic synthesis holding both in unconfused, undivided union — the Chalcedonian pattern as universal grammar.

ranked_frameworks.name: Antifragile Gene Regulatory Networks
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: GRNs repeatedly exposed to transcriptional noise don't merely tolerate it — they reconfigure their attractor landscape to expand the repertoire of stable expression states, gaining regulatory fitness from disorder.

ranked_frameworks.name: Wu Wei Reinforcement Learning (WWRL)
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: An RL agent that minimizes policy-dynamics divergence alongside reward converges faster and generalizes better, because aligned actions exploit the MDP's natural transition structure rather than fighting it.

ranked_frameworks.name: Strategic Hormesis
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Firms that absorb small competitive shocks within a narrow intensity window activate adaptive capabilities that produce greater long-term resilience than shielded firms — too little threat breeds complacency, too much triggers death spiral.

ranked_frameworks.name: Epidemiological Occupancy Threshold Model
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Disease transmission in structured populations follows dose-response dynamics where the effective reproductive number depends not just on contact rate but on a threshold occupancy of susceptibles in spatial compartments — below a critical local density, transmission stalls even when aggregate R0 exceeds 1.

ranked_frameworks.name: Pedogenic Fermentation Selection Model
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Soil formation proceeds through microbial fermentation-like stages where organic matter is transformed by sequential metabolic guilds, each conditioning the substrate for the next — and the fitness of a soil horizon is its capacity to sustain this metabolic relay across wet-dry cycles.

ranked_frameworks.name: Doctrinal Attachment Theory
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Judicial systems form 'internal working models' of legal reasoning through early formative precedents, and the responsiveness pattern of higher courts to lower court decisions determines the long-term coherence and stability of an entire doctrinal lineage — mirroring how caregiver responsiveness shapes an infant's relational blueprint.

ranked_frameworks.name: CapacityRank
ranked_frameworks.core_claim: Road segments can be ranked by congestion-inducing potential using a PageRank-like stationary distribution over route flows; high-CapacityRank segments are where adding capacity most strongly attracts latent demand and restores equilibrium congestion, because they sit at convergence points of many probable commuter chains.

ranked_frameworks.name: Prospect Virtue Theory
